lmp:Semantics.lmp:Comment: Reduces the level of nuclear PKM isoform M2 which results in repression of cyclin CCND1 transcription and reduced cell growth

Interacts with NRBP1 (PubMed:22510880). Interacts with PKM isoform M2; the interaction results in reduced nuclear levels of PKM isoform M2, leading to repression of cyclin CCND1 transcription and reduced cell growth (PubMed:27573352). Interacts with WDR77 (PubMed:27337956)
May be involved in the loss of cell cycle regulation in colorectal tumors, expression is shown to be decreased in the majority of tumors

lmp:CrossReference.lmp:Property: 0 GO annotations based on evolutionary models
Expressed in buccal mucosa cell and 209 other cell types or tissues
P:regulation of transcription by RNA polymerase II

lmp:Reference.lmp:Citation: Prediction of the coding sequences of unidentified human genes. X. The complete sequences of 100 new cDNA clones from brain which can code for large proteins in vitro.
The status, quality, and expansion of the NIH full-length cDNA project: the Mammalian Gene Collection (MGC).
Identification and characterization of a family of leucine zipper genes related to TSC22.
A quantitative atlas of mitotic phosphorylation.
Lys-N and trypsin cover complementary parts of the phosphoproteome in a refined SCX-based approach.
Initial characterization of the human central proteome.
Nuclear receptor binding protein 1 regulates intestinal progenitor cell homeostasis and tumour formation.
An enzyme assisted RP-RPLC approach for in-depth analysis of human liver phosphoproteome.
TSC22D2 interacts with PKM2 and inhibits cell growth in colorectal cancer.
Yeast two-hybrid screening identified WDR77 as a novel interacting partner of TSC22D2.
